children.heading: Requirements relating to provision of services
children.label: b Requirements relating to provision of services

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: A nursing facility must care for its residents in such a manner and in such an environment as will promote maintenance or enhancement of the quality of life of each resident.

children.heading: Quality assessment and assurance
children.label: B Quality assessment and assurance
children.text: A nursing facility must maintain a quality assessment and assurance committee, consisting of the director of nursing services, a physician designated by the facility, and at least 3 other members of the facility’s staff, which (i) meets at least quarterly to identify issues with respect to which quality assessment and assurance activities are necessary and (ii) develops and implements appropriate plans of action to correct identified quality deficiencies. A State or the Secretary may not require disclosure of the records of such committee except insofar as such disclosure is related to the compliance of such committee with the requirements of this subparagraph.

children.heading: Scope of services and activities under plan of care
children.label: 2 Scope of services and activities under plan of care
A
children.text: describes the medical, nursing, and psychosocial needs of the resident and how such needs will be met;

children.label: B
children.text: is initially prepared, with the participation to the extent practicable of the resident or the resident’s family or legal representative, by a team which includes the resident’s attending physician and a registered professional nurse with responsibility for the resident; and

children.label: C
children.text: is periodically reviewed and revised by such team after each assessment under paragraph (3).

children.label: i
children.text: describes the resident’s capability to perform daily life functions and significant impairments in functional capacity;

children.label: ii
children.text: is based on a uniform minimum data set specified by the Secretary under subsection (f)(6)(A);

children.label: iii
children.text: uses an instrument which is specified by the State under subsection (e)(5); and

children.label: iv
children.text: includes the identification of medical problems.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Each such assessment must be conducted or coordinated (with the appropriate participation of health professionals) by a registered professional nurse who signs and certifies the completion of the assessment. Each individual who completes a portion of such an assessment shall sign and certify as to the accuracy of that portion of the assessment.

children.label: I
children.text: An individual who willfully and knowingly certifies under clause (i) a material and false statement in a resident assessment is subject to a civil money penalty of not more than $1,000 with respect to each assessment.

children.label: II
children.text: An individual who willfully and knowingly causes another individual to certify under clause (i) a material and false statement in a resident assessment is subject to a civil money penalty of not more than $5,000 with respect to each assessment.

children.label: III
children.text: The provisions of (other than subsections (a) and (b)) shall apply to a civil money penalty under this clause in the same manner as such provisions apply to a penalty or proceeding under .section 1320a–7a of this titlesection 1320a–7a(a) of this title

children.heading: Use of independent assessors
children.label: iii Use of independent assessors
children.text: If a State determines, under a survey under subsection (g) or otherwise, that there has been a knowing and willful certification of false assessments under this paragraph, the State may require (for a period specified by the State) that resident assessments under this paragraph be conducted and certified by individuals who are independent of the facility and who are approved by the State.

children.label: I
children.text: promptly upon (but no later than 14 days after the date of) admission for each individual admitted on or after , and by not later than , for each resident of the facility on that date;October 1, 1990October 1, 1991

children.label: II
children.text: promptly after a significant change in the resident’s physical or mental condition; and

children.label: III
children.text: in no case less often than once every 12 months.

children.heading: Resident review
children.label: ii Resident review
children.text: The nursing facility must examine each resident no less frequently than once every 3 months and, as appropriate, revise the resident’s assessment to assure the continuing accuracy of the assessment.

children.heading: Use
children.label: D Use
children.text: The results of such an assessment shall be used in developing, reviewing, and revising the resident’s plan of care under paragraph (2).

children.heading: Coordination
children.label: E Coordination
children.text: Such assessments shall be coordinated with any State-required preadmission screening program to the maximum extent practicable in order to avoid duplicative testing and effort. In addition, a nursing facility shall notify the State mental health authority or State mental retardation or developmental disability authority, as applicable, promptly after a significant change in the physical or mental condition of a resident who is mentally ill or mentally retarded.

children.heading: Requirements relating to preadmission screening for mentally ill and mentally retarded individuals
children.label: F Requirements relating to preadmission screening for mentally ill and mentally retarded individuals
i
children.text: is mentally ill (as defined in subsection (e)(7)(G)(i)) unless the State mental health authority has determined (based on an independent physical and mental evaluation performed by a person or entity other than the State mental health authority) prior to admission that, because of the physical and mental condition of the individual, the individual requires the level of services provided by a nursing facility, and, if the individual requires such level of services, whether the individual requires specialized services for mental illness, or

children.label: ii
children.text: is mentally retarded (as defined in subsection (e)(7)(G)(ii)) unless the State mental retardation or developmental disability authority has determined prior to admission that, because of the physical and mental condition of the individual, the individual requires the level of services provided by a nursing facility, and, if the individual requires such level of services, whether the individual requires specialized services for mental retardation.

children.label: i
children.text: nursing and related services and specialized rehabilitative services to attain or maintain the highest practicable physical, mental, and psychosocial well-being of each resident;

children.label: ii
children.text: medically-related social services to attain or maintain the highest practicable physical, mental, and psychosocial well-being of each resident;

children.label: iii
children.text: pharmaceutical services (including procedures that assure the accurate acquiring, receiving, dispensing, and administering of all drugs and biologicals) to meet the needs of each resident;

children.label: iv
children.text: dietary services that assure that the meals meet the daily nutritional and special dietary needs of each resident;

children.label: v
children.text: an on-going program, directed by a qualified professional, of activities designed to meet the interests and the physical, mental, and psychosocial well-being of each resident;

children.label: vi
children.text: routine dental services (to the extent covered under the State plan) and emergency dental services to meet the needs of each resident; and

children.label: vii
children.text: treatment and services required by mentally ill and mentally retarded residents not otherwise provided or arranged for (or required to be provided or arranged for) by the State.

children.heading: Qualified persons providing services
children.label: B Qualified persons providing services
children.text: Services described in clauses (i), (ii), (iii), (iv), and (vi) of subparagraph (A) must be provided by qualified persons in accordance with each resident’s written plan of care.

children.heading: Required nursing care; facility waivers
children.label: C Required nursing care; facility waivers
I
children.text: except as provided in clause (ii), must provide 24-hour licensed nursing services which are sufficient to meet the nursing needs of its residents, and

children.label: II
children.text: except as provided in clause (ii), must use the services of a registered professional nurse for at least 8 consecutive hours a day, 7 days a week.

children.label: I
children.text: the facility demonstrates to the satisfaction of the State that the facility has been unable, despite diligent efforts (including offering wages at the community prevailing rate for nursing facilities), to recruit appropriate personnel,

children.label: II
children.text: the State determines that a waiver of the requirement will not endanger the health or safety of individuals staying in the facility,

children.label: III
children.text: the State finds that, for any such periods in which licensed nursing services are not available, a registered professional nurse or a physician is obligated to respond immediately to telephone calls from the facility,

children.label: IV
children.text: the State agency granting a waiver of such requirements provides notice of the waiver to the State long-term care ombudsman (established under section 307(a)(12) of the Older Americans Act of 1965) and the protection and advocacy system in the State for the mentally ill and the mentally retarded, and1See References in Text note below.1

children.label: V
children.text: the nursing facility that is granted such a waiver by a State notifies residents of the facility (or, where appropriate, the guardians or legal representatives of such residents) and members of their immediate families of the waiver.

children.heading: Assumption of waiver authority by Secretary
children.label: iii Assumption of waiver authority by Secretary
children.text: If the Secretary determines that a State has shown a clear pattern and practice of allowing waivers in the absence of diligent efforts by facilities to meet the staffing requirements, the Secretary shall assume and exercise the authority of the State to grant waivers.

children.text: has completed a training and competency evaluation program, or a competency evaluation program, approved by the State under subsection (e)(1)(A), and

children.label: II
children.text: is competent to provide nursing or nursing-related services.

children.label: ii
children.text: A nursing facility must not use on a temporary, per diem, leased, or on any other basis other than as a permanent employee any individual as a nurse aide in the facility on or after , unless the individual meets the requirements described in clause (i).January 1, 1991

children.heading: Offering competency evaluation programs for current employees
children.label: B Offering competency evaluation programs for current employees
children.text: A nursing facility must provide, for individuals used as a nurse aide by the facility as of , for a competency evaluation program approved by the State under subsection (e)(1) and such preparation as may be necessary for the individual to complete such a program by .January 1, 1990October 1, 1990

children.heading: Competency
children.label: C Competency
children.text: The nursing facility must not permit an individual, other than in a training and competency evaluation program approved by the State, to serve as a nurse aide or provide services of a type for which the individual has not demonstrated competency and must not use such an individual as a nurse aide unless the facility has inquired of any State registry established under subsection (e)(2)(A) that the facility believes will include information concerning the individual.

children.heading: Re-training required
children.label: D Re-training required
children.text: For purposes of subparagraph (A), if, since an individual’s most recent completion of a training and competency evaluation program, there has been a continuous period of 24 consecutive months during none of which the individual performed nursing or nursing-related services for monetary compensation, such individual shall complete a new training and competency evaluation program, or a new competency evaluation program.

children.heading: Regular in-service education
children.label: E Regular in-service education
children.text: The nursing facility must provide such regular performance review and regular in-service education as assures that individuals used as nurse aides are competent to perform services as nurse aides, including training for individuals providing nursing and nursing-related services to residents with cognitive impairments.

children.label: i
children.text: who is a licensed health professional (as defined in subparagraph (G)) or a registered dietician, or

children.label: ii
children.text: who volunteers to provide such services without monetary compensation.

children.heading: Licensed health professional defined
children.label: G Licensed health professional defined
children.text: In this paragraph, the term “licensed health professional” means a physician, physician assistant, nurse practitioner, physical, speech, or occupational therapist, physical or occupational therapy assistant, registered professional nurse, licensed practical nurse, or licensed or certified social worker.

children.label: A
children.text: require that the health care of every resident be provided under the supervision of a physician (or, at the option of a State, under the supervision of a nurse practitioner, clinical nurse specialist, or physician assistant who is not an employee of the facility but who is working in collaboration with a physician);

children.label: B
children.text: provide for having a physician available to furnish necessary medical care in case of emergency; and

children.label: C
children.text: maintain clinical records on all residents, which records include the plans of care (described in paragraph (2)) and the residents’ assessments (described in paragraph (3)), as well as the results of any pre-admission screening conducted under subsection (e)(7).

children.heading: Required social services
children.label: 7 Required social services
children.text: In the case of a nursing facility with more than 120 beds, the facility must have at least one social worker (with at least a bachelor’s degree in social work or similar professional qualifications) employed full-time to provide or assure the provision of social services.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: A nursing facility shall post daily for each shift the current number of licensed and unlicensed nursing staff directly responsible for resident care in the facility. The information shall be displayed in a uniform manner (as specified by the Secretary) and in a clearly visible place.

children.heading: Publication of data
children.label: B Publication of data
children.text: A nursing facility shall, upon request, make available to the public the nursing staff data described in subparagraph (A).

children.heading: Requirements relating to residents’ rights
children.label: c Requirements relating to residents’ rights

children.heading: Free choice
children.label: i Free choice
children.text: The right to choose a personal attending physician, to be fully informed in advance about care and treatment, to be fully informed in advance of any changes in care or treatment that may affect the resident’s well-being, and (except with respect to a resident adjudged incompetent) to participate in planning care and treatment or changes in care and treatment.

children.label: I
children.text: to ensure the physical safety of the resident or other residents, and

children.label: II
children.text: only upon the written order of a physician that specifies the duration and circumstances under which the restraints are to be used (except in emergency circumstances specified by the Secretary until such an order could reasonably be obtained).

children.heading: Privacy
children.label: iii Privacy
children.text: The right to privacy with regard to accommodations, medical treatment, written and telephonic communications, visits, and meetings of family and of resident groups.

children.heading: Confidentiality
children.label: iv Confidentiality
children.text: The right to confidentiality of personal and clinical records and to access to current clinical records of the resident upon request by the resident or the resident’s legal representative, within 24 hours (excluding hours occurring during a weekend or holiday) after making such a request.

children.label: I
children.text: to reside and receive services with reasonable accommodation of individual needs and preferences, except where the health or safety of the individual or other residents would be endangered, and

children.label: II
children.text: to receive notice before the room or roommate of the resident in the facility is changed.

children.heading: Grievances
children.label: vi Grievances
children.text: The right to voice grievances with respect to treatment or care that is (or fails to be) furnished, without discrimination or reprisal for voicing the grievances and the right to prompt efforts by the facility to resolve grievances the resident may have, including those with respect to the behavior of other residents.

children.heading: Participation in resident and family groups
children.label: vii Participation in resident and family groups
children.text: The right of the resident to organize and participate in resident groups in the facility and the right of the resident’s family to meet in the facility with the families of other residents in the facility.

children.heading: Participation in other activities
children.label: viii Participation in other activities
children.text: The right of the resident to participate in social, religious, and community activities that do not interfere with the rights of other residents in the facility.

children.heading: Examination of survey results
children.label: ix Examination of survey results
children.text: The right to examine, upon reasonable request, the results of the most recent survey of the facility conducted by the Secretary or a State with respect to the facility and any plan of correction in effect with respect to the facility.

children.heading: Refusal of certain transfers
children.label: x Refusal of certain transfers
children.text: The right to refuse a transfer to another room within the facility, if a purpose of the transfer is to relocate the resident from a portion of the facility that is not a skilled nursing facility (for purposes of subchapter XVIII) to a portion of the facility that is such a skilled nursing facility.

children.heading: Other rights
children.label: xi Other rights
children.text: Any other right established by the Secretary.

children.label: i
children.text: inform each resident, orally and in writing at the time of admission to the facility, of the resident’s legal rights during the stay at the facility and of the requirements and procedures for establishing eligibility for medical assistance under this subchapter, including the right to request an assessment under ;section 1396r–5(c)(1)(B) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: make available to each resident, upon reasonable request, a written statement of such rights (which statement is updated upon changes in such rights) including the notice (if any) of the State developed under subsection (e)(6);

children.label: I
children.text: at the time of admission to the facility or, if later, at the time the resident becomes eligible for such assistance, of the items and services (including those specified under ) that are included in nursing facility services under the State plan and for which the resident may not be charged (except as permitted in section 1396 of this title), and of those other items and services that the facility offers and for which the resident may be charged and the amount of the charges for such items and services, andsection 1396a(a)(28)(B) of this titleo

children.label: II
children.text: of changes in the items and services described in subclause (I) and of changes in the charges imposed for items and services described in that subclause; and

children.label: iv
children.text: inform each other resident, in writing before or at the time of admission and periodically during the resident’s stay, of services available in the facility and of related charges for such services, including any charges for services not covered under subchapter XVIII or by the facility’s basic per diem charge.

children.heading: Rights of incompetent residents
children.label: C Rights of incompetent residents
children.text: In the case of a resident adjudged incompetent under the laws of a State, the rights of the resident under this subchapter shall devolve upon, and, to the extent judged necessary by a court of competent jurisdiction, be exercised by, the person appointed under State law to act on the resident’s behalf.

children.heading: Use of psychopharmacologic drugs
children.label: D Use of psychopharmacologic drugs
children.text: Psychopharmacologic drugs may be administered only on the orders of a physician and only as part of a plan (included in the written plan of care described in paragraph (2)) designed to eliminate or modify the symptoms for which the drugs are prescribed and only if, at least annually an independent, external consultant reviews the appropriateness of the drug plan of each resident receiving such drugs.

children.text: the facility ceases to operate.
